message:
fix(fuzz,crypt): bound ptrace fuzz buffer + tidy md5 test slice

Two unrelated small cleanups grouped together because each is
trivially scoped and not worth its own commit.

fuzz/fuzz_ptrace.rs:
- New const MAX_OUT_BUF_BYTES = 256.
- The fuzzer now early-returns if the libfuzzer-sys input claims an
  out_buf_bytes longer than 256. The old harness happily passed
  every input length straight to the ptrace dispatcher, so a
  fuzzer-corpus entry that landed on an enormous out_buf_bytes
  would dominate runtime / RSS for that case before the membrane
  rejected it. 256 bytes still exercises every documented PTRACE_*
  request path; bigger values were noise.
- No code outside the fuzz target is touched; this only changes
  what the fuzz corpus can submit.

core/crypt/md5.rs:
- output_body_uses_only_crypt_base64_alphabet test changed
    for &b in h[body_start..].as_bytes() { ... }
  to
    for &b in &h.as_bytes()[body_start..] { ... }
  Functionally identical (str slicing + as_bytes is byte-identical
  to as_bytes + slice), but the new form is one less UTF-8
  boundary round-trip and matches the surrounding crate style. No
  product code change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
